class echo.objectives.DemandCharge(*, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, rate: pydantic.types.PositiveFloat, min_demand: float = 0.0, window_array: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, List]] = None, window_object: echo.objectives.Window = None, reset_periods: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, List]] = None, import_demand: bool = False, export_demand: bool = False, window_active: str = None, num_reset_periods: int = None, reset_index: echo.objectives.pyomo.core.base.set.RangeSet = None, max_demand_val: str = None)

Bases: pydantic.main.BaseModel

A demand charge is a rate that applies to the maximum demand over one or many specified time windows.

class echo.objectives.ExportTariff(*, component: echo.echo_models.Port, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, tariff_array: Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list], expansion_periods: pydantic.types.PositiveInt = 1, export_tariff: str = None, export_tariff_dict: dict = None)

Bases: echo.objectives.Tariff

The ExportTariff objective applies a tariff, defined as an array of prices, to the negative (exporting) component of the specified port.

Bases: echo.objectives.Tariff

The ImportTariff objective applies a price per kWh of energy imported at a defined port.

class echo.objectives.PathTariff(*, component: echo.echo_models.Path, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, tariff_array: Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list], expansion_periods: pydantic.types.PositiveInt = 1, path_tariff: str = None, path_tariff_dict: dict = None)

Bases: echo.objectives.Tariff

The PathTariff objective applies a cost per kW of power flow on a specified path.

objective_expr(model)

class echo.objectives.PeakNegativePower(*, component: Optional[Union[echo.echo_models.Port, echo.echo_models.Path]] = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, max_neg: str = None)

Bases: echo.objectives.Objective

The PeakNegativePower objective minimises the peak negative (exported) power at the specified port.

apply_constraints(model)
create_vars(model)
max_neg: Optional[str]
objective_expr(model)
class echo.objectives.PeakPositivePower(*, component: echo.echo_models.Port, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, max_pos: str = None)

Bases: echo.objectives.Objective

The PeakPositivePower objective minimises the peak positive (imported) power at the specified port.

apply_constraints(model)
component: echo.echo_models.Port
create_vars(model)
max_pos: Optional[str]

class echo.objectives.QuadraticPower(*, component: echo.echo_models.Port, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None)

Bases: echo.objectives.Objective

The QuadraticPower objective minimises flow^2 at a specified port.

component: echo.echo_models.Port
objective_expr(model)

class echo.objectives.ThroughputCost(*, component: echo.echo_models.Port, uid: uuid.UUID = None, name: str = None, rate: pydantic.types.PositiveFloat)

Bases: echo.objectives.Objective

A ThroughputCost objective applies a fixed rate to total throughput (i.e. import minus export) at a port.

apply_constraints(model)
component: echo.echo_models.Port
objective_expr(model)
rate: pydantic.types.PositiveFloat

day_type: List[echo.objectives.Day]
end_time: datetime.time
overlaps(other: echo.objectives.TimePeriod)

Determine whether two `TimePeriod`s are overlapping. Used for validating `Window`s. :param other:

Returns:

start_time: datetime.time
to_bool(df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) → numpy.ndarray

Convert time period to an array based on whether a row is in the time period :param df: input DataFrame

Returns:

class echo.objectives.Window(*, time_periods: List[echo.objectives.TimePeriod], reset_periods: echo.objectives.ResetPeriod = ResetPeriod.year)

Bases: pydantic.main.BaseModel

Class for specifying window over which a tariff is calculated

get_reset_period_array(df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) → list

Returns an array where each entry is the number of time steps within which the demand charge is calculated.

classmethod non_overlapping_periods(v)
reset_periods: Optional[echo.objectives.ResetPeriod]
time_periods: List[echo.objectives.TimePeriod]

To convert a window to a bool array
